title: [redacted for blind review]

largest_top_level_subdiscussion.root_excerpt: I notice the term `folx' showing up everywhere recently. Anyone actually use it in real life or is this just an online thing?

in_scope_nodes.excerpt: I have never seen anyone use folx outside of online forums.
This is just Twitter folx-talk, nobody says it irl.
It's a corporate-DEI thing — I don't think anyone outside HR communications actually uses it.
In my whole career I've heard `folx' spoken once.
10 years in tech and I've literally never heard a coworker say it out loud.
I use it constantly — most of my Slack does too.
It's used widely in the queer-friendly spaces I'm in.
Heard it from co-workers all the time — depends what your team is like.
Folx > folks visually anyway, the x just looks better.
Reminds me of the `latinx' debate from 2018.

note: Excerpts and pseudonymous IDs are reconstructions for the demo. Real episode content is in the released benchmark; this demo uses paraphrased equivalents to illustrate the verification protocol without surfacing original user content.
